        logger.info(f"Starting SAP Retriever Agent on {host}:{port}")
        server.start()
        logger.info(f"Uvicorn should now be running at http://{host}:{port}")

    except MissingAPIKeyError as e:
        logger.error(f"API Key missing: {e}")
        exit(1)
    except Exception as e:
        logger.error(f"Startup error: {e}")
        exit(1)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

Key Functions and Design:

  • Agent Card Declaration: The agent's public metadata is encapsulated in an AgentCard, including name, URL, supported modalities, skills (e.g., answering SAP-related queries), and version. This enables discovery by client agents via the /.well-known/agent.json endpoint.

  • Skill Definition: The agent declares a single skill (sap_agent) describing its domain expertise: SAP product knowledge retrieval.

  • Task Manager Binding: The A2AServer is configured with a custom AgentTaskManager that handles A2A task lifecycle and invokes the SAPAgent defined earlier.

  • Streaming Support: Capabilities are declared to indicate the agent supports streaming task updates (i.e., tasks/sendSubscribe), a core feature of A2A.

This entry point allows the agent to be deployed as a compliant A2A Server capable of securely receiving and responding to tasks over HTTP using the shared protocol, ensuring full interoperability with any A2A client.

 

Extending the Agent Mesh: Utility Agent and the Pattern for Domain-Specific Agents

Following the same structure as the SAP Agent, the Utility Agent demonstrates how to create another domain-specific A2A-compatible agent. In this case, one focused on answering weather and time-related queries using external tools.

Just like the SAP Agent, it includes:

  • agent.py – Encapsulates reasoning and tool-calling logic using GenAI Hub + MCP tools (get_weather, get_time_now).
  • task_manager.py – Manages the A2A task lifecycle (receiving, processing, streaming updates).
  • __main__.py – Boots up the agent with a proper Agent Card and A2A server configuration.

The main differences lie in:

  • The tool set (e.g., get_weather vs retriever).

        skills = [
            AgentSkill(
                id="utility_agent",
                name="Utility Agent",
                description="Answers weather and time-related questions using external tools.",
                tags=["weather", "time"],
                examples=[
                    "What time is it in Brazil?", "What is the weather like now?"
                    ]
                )
            ]

  • The instructions returned to the LLM for tool decisioning.

    async def _generate_instruction(self) -> str:
        """Generate dynamic system prompt based on registered tools."""
        description = json.dumps(await self.list_tools(), indent=2)
        return f"""
        You are an intelligent AI assistant capable of deciding whether to invoke tools based on the user's request.

        Available tools:
        {description}

        Instructions:
        - For each relevant tool, return a JSON entry with the function name and parameters.
        - If no tool is relevant, return an entry with decision = "no_tool".

        Return ONLY valid JSON like:
        {{
          "tool_calls": [
            {{
              "decision": "tool",
              "reason": "The user asked for weather.",
              "function": "get_weather",
              "parameters": {{
                "latitude": 48.8566,
                "longitude": 2.3522
              }}
            }},
            {{
              "decision": "tool",
              "reason": "The user asked for time.",
              "function": "get_time_now",
              "parameters": {{}}
            }}
          ]
        }}
        """

  • The skills metadata advertised in the Agent Card (weather, time vs SAP knowledge).

        agent_card = AgentCard(
            name="Utility Agent",
            description="Answers weather and time-related queries using tool results.",
            url=f"http://{host}:{port}/",
            version="1.0.0",
            defaultInputModes=UtilityAgent.SUPPORTED_CONTENT_TYPES,
            defaultOutputModes=UtilityAgent.SUPPORTED_CONTENT_TYPES,
            capabilities=capabilities,
            skills=skills,
        )

This pattern is reusable: to create any new agent for your own use case (be it finance, HR, manufacturing and others) you only need to adapt these same three files with your own domain tools, task logic, and descriptions. All remaining A2A and MCP mechanics remain consistent across agents.

 

Managing Dependencies and Workspaces with pyproject.toml

To standardize how A2A-compatible agents are built, run, and extended, each agent (e.g., SAP Agent, Utility Agent) is configured using a pyproject.toml file. This file handles dependency management, agent-specific metadata, and workspace definitions, making it easy to scale to additional agents later.

[project]
name = "a2a-agent"
version = "0.1.0"
description = "A2A-compatible Agent using MCP and SAP GenAI Hub"
requires-python = ">=3.12"

dependencies = [
  "ag2[mcp, openai]>=0.8.6",              # A2A framework, MCP client, OpenAI-compatible LLM support
  "generative-ai-hub-sdk[all]>=0.4.0",    # SAP GenAI Hub orchestration + LLM schemas
  "fastapi",                              # Required by A2AServer
  "uvicorn[standard]>=0.27.1",            # Local server runner
  "httpx",                                # HTTP + SSE client
  "click>=8.1.3"                          # For CLI option handling
]

[tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel]
packages = ["common"]

[tool.uv.workspace]
members = [
  "agents/sap",
  "agents/utils"
]

[build-system]
requires = ["hatchling"]
build-backend = "hatchling.build"

[dependency-groups]
dev = [
  "pytest>=8.3.5",
  "pytest-mock>=3.14.0",
  "ruff>=0.11.2"
]

  • [project]

    • Defines agent-specific metadata: name, version, description, and Python version.

    • Lists core dependencies for running A2A + MCP + SAP GenAI Hub orchestration:

      • ag2[mcp, openai]: A2A protocol, MCP support, and LLM-compatible interfaces.

      • generative-ai-hub-sdk[all]: SAP’s SDK for orchestration, schema validation, and LLMs.

      • fastapi, uvicorn[standard]: Required for serving A2A HTTP endpoints.

      • httpx: Needed by SSE clients and GenAI Hub HTTP logic.

      • click: Optional but used to simplify CLI startup with __main__.py.

  • [tool.uv.sources]

    • Used by uv for local development. This helps you run each agent individually with uv run ., while still sharing dependencies.

  • [tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel]

    • Ensures shared logic (like the common folder) is included when packaging agents.

  • [tool.uv.workspace]

    • Declares a multi-agent workspace for tools like uv to resolve dependencies across agents (e.g., agents/sap, agents/utils).

  • [build-system]

    • Specifies hatchling as the build backend. This is required for workspace coordination and dependency isolation.

  • [dependency-groups]

    • Optional section to include development tools (e.g., pytest, ruff) if testing and linting are needed.

This setup is highly extensible. To create a new agent, simply:

  1. Copy one of the agent folders (e.g., agents/sap).
  2. Rename and adapt the pyproject.toml.
  3. Update only the agent.py, task_manager.py, and __main__.py.

With this modular approach, you’re ready to scale your agent mesh without reinventing your development setup each time.

 

Running the Agents and Launching the Demo UI

To experience A2A in action, you can either build your own host application or use the official Google A2A Demo UI which is the approach we'll follow in this walkthrough. This web app acts as a visual interface for interacting with your Host and Remote agents, showcasing task orchestration, dynamic responses, and streaming task updates.

Step 1: Start Your A2A-Compatible Agents

First, start your custom agents (like SAP Agent and Utility Agent) locally. For example:

cd agents/sap
uv run .

cd agents/utils
uv run .

Ensure both agents expose valid AgentCards via the /.well-known/agent.json endpoint, and that they are reachable from your browser or backend app.

 

Step 2: Launch Google’s A2A Demo UI

The official A2A Demo Web App provides a conversation-centric UI for testing A2A agent collaboration.

Key Features:
  • Add agents dynamically by pasting their agent.json URL.
  • Talk to one or more agents via the Host Agent.
  • View detailed message/task history and streaming updates.

To get started:

cd demo/ui

Then, set up your environment variables for authentication (in our case, with Google AI Studio):

echo "GOOGLE_API_KEY=your_api_key_here" >> .env

Finally, launch the UI:

uv run main.py

 

Setting Up Agents in the A2A Demo Application

Once both agents are running locally (in our case SAP Agent on localhost:8001 and Utility Agent on localhost:8002), you can use the Google A2A Demo UI to interact with them visually.

Step-by-Step: Register Remote Agents

  1. Access the Agents Page
    Navigate to http://0.0.0.0:12000/agents — this is where you'll register the remote agents.
     

  2. Add a New Remote Agent
    Click the upload icon. A dialog will appear prompting you to enter the Agent Address.
     

     

  3. Register SAP Agent
    Enter localhost:8001 and click Read. The AgentCard metadata will be fetched:

  4. Register Utility Agent
    Repeat the process for the second agent using localhost:8002.

  5. Confirm Agent Registration
    You should now see both agents listed:

     

Scenario-Based Evaluation of A2A Agents

Here are the results of running the same questions as in our previous two blogs, but now using the A2A-enabled multi-agent system with dynamic routing and streaming UI.

"What time is it?"

Handled by: Utility Agent
Analysis:
Compared to Blog #1 (basic agentic orchestration via GenAI Hub) and Blog #2 (MCP + Kyma), this result is functionally the same but here, the user sees it rendered instantly in the UI, and the agent is dynamically plugged in, not hardcoded.

 

"How’s the weather in Paris?"

Handled by: Utility Agent
Analysis:
Same weather tool usage as in the previous blogs but now you see rich streaming and the agent auto-resolved via A2A discovery. You didn’t manually route the query, A2A did.

 

"What is SAP Business Data Cloud?"

Handled by: SAP Agent
Analysis:
As in the previous blogs, this content is LLM-driven with SAP domain context. But now, you see that the LLM inside the SAP Agent was independently maintained and registered, showing how easy it is to compose modular domain agents.

 

Composite Query:

This example captures the power of modular reasoning, where tool-using agents handle real-time or specialized queries while the LLM addresses conceptual prompts, all seamlessly synthesized into a unified response.

"Explain what a blockchain is and also tell me: how’s the weather in Brazil, what time it is now, and what is the role of SAP Datasphere in SAP Business Data Cloud?"

This compound query is intelligently decomposed and delegated behind the scenes:

Tool-Using Agents

  • Weather Agent provides:
    → Weather in Brazil: 30.4°C with a wind speed of 7.6 m/s.

  • Time Agent provides:
    → Current Time: 14:32.

  • SAP Agent provides:
    → SAP Datasphere’s role in SAP Business Data Cloud.

LLM-Only Response

  • Blockchain explanation is handled directly by the base LLM. This shows that not every subtask requires a tool or external call.

This is just a simplified example of how A2A can be implemented. While it doesn’t cover all the architectural considerations, edge cases, or production-level variables, it provides a solid starting point for understanding the core concepts and possibilities.

 

Wrapping Up and Next Steps

In this blog, we expanded our SAP-based agentic architecture by adopting the Agent2Agent (A2A) protocol, an open standard that enables secure, interoperable communication between intelligent agents across teams, platforms, and domains.

Building on our earlier work with SAP Generative AI Hub, Model Context Protocol (MCP), and SAP BTP Kyma, we showcased how to scale beyond a single assistant by composing a mesh of specialized, A2A-compliant agents: each with its own skills, responsibilities, and autonomy. From retrieving SAP knowledge via the SAP Agent, to answering time and weather queries via the Utility Agent, every agent is discoverable, modular, and orchestrated via a central Host Agent.

This A2A-powered setup unlocks true composability and reuse in enterprise AI: agents are no longer hardcoded or monolithic, but instead self-describing, interoperable services that can be reused across use cases and extended independently.

Now it’s your turn to take this further.

  • Connect your multi-agent system to real SAP business data and enterprise tools.
  • Showcase high-impact use cases such as document summarization, financial analysis, planning support etc.
  • Explore how to monitor, secure, and govern your agents in production using SAP BTP.

With A2A and MCP in place, you have the foundation for a scalable, modular AI architecture that is ready to grow with your enterprise needs.

Hope that helps!

 

Further References

  • Source Code: GitHub repository
  • Agent2Agent (A2A) Protocol
  • Agent2Agent (A2A) Protocol Repository
  • Model Context Protocol (MCP)
  • Model Context Protocol (MCP) Repository
  • SAP Libraries and SDKs
  • Generative AI Hub SDK
  • Orchestration

entry.title: Sapphire Orlando 2025: Explore business scenarios enabled by SAP BTP that you can implement today
entry.content:
Hello, SAP Community!

My name is Joanne Koh-Stewart, and I am Director of Product Marketing for SAP BTP for Business Applications and Core. I focus on identifying and nurturing SAP BTP use cases that solve real customer challenges across the Business Suite and lines of business. You might have seen my name around Sapphire and TechEd times, writing about our SAP BTP use cases.

I'm pleased to announce that I'll be presenting again at this year's Sapphire Orlando. My session Explore business scenarios enabled by SAP BTP that you can implement today with my colleague @denisema, is scheduled for Tuesday, May 20th at 11:30am – 11:50am EDT.

 

What you will learn about:

This session is particularly exciting because it allows me to share insights gained from our customers and partners who leveraged SAP BTP. The idea for this session emerged from numerous conversations with customers eager to understand what they could do with SAP BTP and how they could quickly implement it to address their pressing business challenges. We've curated a selection of use cases that demonstrate tangible value and can be rapidly deployed.  

For customers who want to try out SAP BTP but are not ready to commit, this session will dive into how customers have different options to get started for free with SAP BTP.

 

We look forward to seeing you:

Whether you're well-versed in SAP BTP or just beginning to explore its potential, this session promises valuable takeaways on business challenges that SAP BTP can help resolve. Denise and I look forward to meeting you at the session or at the BTP for Business Application booth at Sapphire Orlando, and engaging in insightful discussions about the transformative power of SAP BTP and how it can address your specific business needs. Make sure to add Explore business scenarios enabled by SAP BTP that you can implement today session to your Favourites to add it to your Sapphire agenda. 

 

Other related sessions I recommend:  

Mission possible: Accelerate transformation with SAP Discovery Center - BTP2467 

Related community managed tags you should subscribe to remain informed about this topic: 

  • SAP Business Technology Platform
  • SAP Build
  • SAP Business Suite
  • SAP Integration Suite

Related community topic pages: 

  •  SAP Business Technology Platform In Action

 

Now let's hear from you:

  • How are you currently driving digital transformation within your organization?
  • What are your main goals for using SAP BTP?
  • What are/were your biggest challenges in getting started with SAP BTP?

Tell us in the comments, and we look forward to seeing you virtual and/or in Orlando in a few weeks!

entry.title: SAP Business AI - Where to start?
entry.content:
What is SAP Discovery Center?

SAP Discovery Center is a dynamic online platform that serves as your primary resource for exploring and implementing SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) services and AI features. It provides structured guidance for creating tailored business solutions that address specific organizational challenges.

As stated on the platform itself, SAP Discovery Center positions itself as "Your Gateway to Innovation," helping users to "Discover, evaluate, and adopt SAP Business Technology Platform services and AI features to create tailored business solutions."

Key Components of SAP Discovery Center

The Discovery Center is organized around three core pillars:

1. Discover

Browse comprehensive catalogs of:

  • AI Features
  • SAP BTP Services
  • Reference Architectures
  • Customer Stories
  • Solution Diagrams
  • Roadmaps

2. Evaluate

Identify and assess the most suitable solutions with tools like:

  • Service Estimator
  • AI Estimator
  • Price and Value Estimates

3. Adopt

Implement solutions with:

  • Quick Account Setup
  • Proof of Concept Creation
  • Expert Collaboration
  • Initial Setup Guidance
  • Guided Project Board

Featured Content in SAP Discovery Center

The Discovery Center showcases innovation highlights designed to accelerate business transformation:

Missions

Step-by-step guidance for implementing specific use cases, with support from topic experts and the SAP Community. AI Missions:

  1. Get Started with SAP Business AI
  2. Activate Joule for SAP SuccessFactors
  3. GenAI Mail Insights: Develop a CAP application using GenAI and Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG)
  4. Establish a Unified Joule Instance
  5. Activate Joule with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  6. Digitizing Criticality Assessments using SAP AI Core and SAP AI Launchpad
  7. Facilitate Invoice Validation - Leveraging Document Information Extraction
  8. Get Started with SAP Build Code and Joule using Generative AI (Trial and Enterprise Account)
  9. Process and approve your invoices with SAP Build Process Automation
  10. Leveraging SAC visualizations with the power of GenAI
  11. Reduce your CO2 footprint using a smart Generative AI application on SAP BTP
  12. Building Intelligent Data Applications with SAP HANA Cloud Knowledge Graphs
  13. Set Up SAP AI Core Generative AI Hub
     

Reference Architectures

Explore and understand different architecture patterns implementable using SAP Business Technology Platform services, such as:

  • Retrieval Augmented Generation with GenAI on SAP BTP

SAP BTP Services

Discover services that help integrate, extend, and build solutions, including:

  • Integration Suite: An industry-leading integration platform-as-a-service

AI Features Catalog

A comprehensive catalog of the 210 AI capabilities to optimize productivity, drive innovation, and personalize experiences across SAP's product portfolio.

  • Customer Relationship Management: 55 items
  • Enterprise Management (Corporate): 36 items
  • Human Capital Management: 23 items
  • Product Lifecycle Management: 3 items
  • Spend Management: 33 items
  • Supply Chain Management: 31 items
  • Technology Platform: 30 items

The AI Feature Estimator

One of the most valuable tools within the Discovery Center is the AI Feature Estimator. This tool helps organizations:

  • Generate estimates for AI features
  • Modify estimates as requirements evolve
  • Share estimates with stakeholders
  • Understand the potential business impact of AI implementation

Related Resources

SAP Discovery Center connects users to a broader ecosystem of resources:

  • SAP Help Portal
  • SAP BTP Guidance Framework
  • Business Content
  • Customer References
  • Tutorials
  • Communities
  • Road Map Explorer
  • Partner Applications

Why SAP Discovery Center Matters for AI Implementation

For organizations interested in implementing AI within their SAP landscape, the Discovery Center provides several unique advantages:

  1. Centralized Information: A single source of truth for all available AI features across the SAP portfolio
  2. Guided Implementation: Step-by-step missions for specific AI use cases
  3. Estimation Tools: Resources to calculate potential costs and value
  4. Real-world Examples: Customer stories demonstrating successful implementations
  5. Expert Support: Access to topic experts and community resources

By leveraging the SAP Discovery Center, organizations can accelerate their AI adoption journey, reduce implementation risks, and maximize the business value of their investments in SAP's AI capabilities.

 

The Importance of Artificial Intelligence Training for SAP Solutions

Artificial Intelligence (AI) is transforming how companies operate, enabling greater efficiency, process automation, and more accurate predictive analytics. Within the SAP ecosystem, AI plays a fundamental role in optimizing business processes, from financial management to customer experience.

Building AI expertise within the SAP context is not just a competitive advantage but a necessity for professionals who wish to drive innovation and extract maximum value from technological solutions.

Whether through predictive modeling, robotic process automation (RPA), or virtual assistants, AI in the SAP universe is opening new business possibilities. Therefore, investing in continuous learning and exploring specific training can be decisive for standing out in the market.

Exploring the SAP Discovery Center: Strategic Positioning in Artificial Intelligence

The SAP Discovery Center is an essential resource for any professional seeking to understand and implement Artificial Intelligence solutions in the SAP ecosystem. It offers a comprehensive view of use cases, integrations, and best practices, helping companies accelerate their digital journey with AI.

Through the Discovery Center, it's possible to access reference projects, explore services, and gain valuable insights on how to leverage AI capabilities within SAP solutions. This way, professionals and organizations can make more informed decisions and improve their innovation strategy.

List of Artificial Intelligence Training for SAP

Here is an organized list of essential training programs on Artificial Intelligence in SAP:

  1. Exploring the SAP Business AI Portfolio
  2. Introducing SAP Business AI Commercial Model
  3. Introducing SAP Business AI for Cloud ERP
  4. Discovering SAP Business AI Capabilities for SAP Finance
  5. Introducing SAP Business AI for SAP SuccessFactors
  6. Introducing SAP Business AI for Customer Experience
  7. Positioning the Capabilities and Benefits of the SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine
  8. Introducing SAP Business AI for SAP Digital Supply Chain
  9. Introducing SAP Business AI for Spend Management
  10. Applying a Human-Centered Approach to Identify and Define Business AI Use Cases
  11. Navigating Large Language Models Fundamentals and Techniques for Your Use Case
  12. Boosting Your Cloud Transformation Journey with SAP Business AI and Generative AI
  13. Solving Your Business Problems Using Prompts and LLMs in SAP's Generative AI Hub
  14. Provisioning and Implementing Joule
  15. Creating Applications and Extensions Using SAP Build Code
  16. SAP Build Code - Generative AI-powered Development with Joule Copilot
  17. Generative AI with SAP AI Launchpad
  18. SAP HANA Cloud with Vector Engine and GenAI Hub

These training programs are fundamental for any professional who wants to master Artificial Intelligence in the SAP ecosystem and enhance their business solutions.

 
Send your doubts!
